content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 3, 1988

Mr. James Thompson
10014 S. Grevillea Ave.
Inglewood, CA 90304

Re: Your Correspondence To The
Governor Regarding Campaign
Finance Reform
Our File No. G-88-143

Dear Mr. Thompson:

The Governor's Office has forwarded your letter to this
agency for a response. You have expressed your concern over
the level of political action committee (PAC) involvement in
financing election campaigns.

You should be aware that in the June primary election there
are two measures which would impose some restrictions on
campaign contributions. To assist the public in understanding
the provisions of these two measures, the Fair Political
Practices Commission has just released a comparative analysis.
We enclose a copy of that analysis for your benefit.

Thank you for your interest in the issue of campaign
financing. The Fair Political Practices Commission has
produced a number of reports on campaign financing over the
years, including a 1980 report on The California PAC
Phenomenon. If you are interested in receiving copies of that
report or others, please contact the Commission's Technical
Assistance and Analysis Division.

Sincerely,

(Robert E. Leidigh)
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

cc: Governor's Office

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Mr James Thompson
10014 S Grevillea Ave
Inglewood CA 90304

ADVERTISER
DENKMEDIAN
YOUR QUESTIONNAIRE ON NATIONAL
AND STATE ISSUES — IT IS OUR
SENSING THAT YOU HAVE YOUR HANDS
FULL TO OVER FLOWING WITH STATE
ISSUES. I AM SENDING FOUR ISSUES
THAT YOU SHOULD BE INVOLVED IN.
THESE FOUR ISSUES WHEN TALKED
ABOUT IN PUBLIC THE PEOPLE GET VERY
ANGRY THEY WANT SOMETHING DONE
ABOUT IT.

Thankfully,
James Thompson

PRO LIFE AGAINST BACH
ITS A DISGRACE

THAT OUR CALIFORNIA STATE
LEGISLATURE AND ASSEMBLY
ON THE AUCTION FLOOR ALL CON
GLADSTO THE HIGHEST BIDDERS!

I WANT MY STATE SENATE
AND ASSEMBLY SUPPORTED AND ASSENIBLY MAY
AID, ASSEMBLED AND ASSIST
THAT NOT RETURNS THAT WILL
THE VEHICLE INFLUENCE OF HAVE
ON OUR REPRESENTATIVE.
(2) THE WAY TO THE RICH
[illegible] THE DICTATOR [illegible] FAUN.

American values, pride
not what they used to be

Editor:
I am not alone when I say the values
our country once had are now a dim
memory.

In my war, we had the policy of
"unconditional surrender." We
Americans could say in 1945, "We won
the war."

My father was wounded and gassed
on the Western Front in 1918. He
earned the Silver Star, the Purple
Heart and the Victory Medal. He
could say in 1919, "We won the war."

No Americans can say that about
any war, or anything else, since 1946.
What can we say today?

We can say our government starts
its immigration amnesty program May
5. Know what May 5 is?

It's Cinco de Mayo. Welcome illegal
aliens. You can have our country. We
forgot what it feels like to be real
American men.

--BILL DREWS
San Pedro

Los Angeles Times                                    * Friday, March 13, 1987, Part 1      5

Special Interests a Large
Source of Brown's Income

ITEM:
SACRAMENTO—Assembly
Speaker Willie Brown (D-San
Francisco) received at least
$124,000 in income and gifts last
year from special interests that had
business before the Legislature,
according to official documents and
interviews.

ITEM:
The Speaker recently told reporters: "I would hope that my
standard of conduct would be
something that others may wish to
emulate, but I don't wish to be put
in the position of being the keeper
of the morals of other members."

ITEM:
While Brown has been receiving
considerable outside income, he has
also become a leading advocate of
forbidding lawmakers from receiving any outside income. He said he
will sponsor a bill that would ban
outside income for legislators while
raising their annual salaries from
$47,065 to as much as $85,000 a
year.

ITEM:
Unlike the many thousands of
dollars lawmakers receive in campaign contributions, all of the
speaking fees, other outside income
and gifts they accept go directly into their own pockets.

ITEM:
"I did not know of any business
my clients had before the Legislature," Brown said. "When I do, I
disqualify myself. There is no way
to ask everybody."

Brown said he did not
abstain from participating in CA
votes on any legislation last year

PAYMENTS TO SPEAKER WILLIE BROWN

Speaker Willie Brown has reported accepting more
than $270,000 last year in legal fees, gifts and
honorariums. Records show some of this money came
from entities that had dealings with the state. Here is a
partial listing:

[DONOR] [CONTRIBUTION] [WHAT WAS REPORTED]

Santa Fe
Southern Pacific
Corp.

Paid Brown $58,000 in legal
fees.

Reported lobbying legislation on
29 bills on air transportation and
toxic waste issues. The
company also opposed state
plans to build a prison near East
Los Angeles on land it owns.

The Assn. of
California
Insurance Cos.

Gave Brown $4,795.82 tip to
$6,000, including air fare, hotel,
meals, theater tickets and
ground transportation.

Reported lobbying legislation on
more than 150 bills affecting the
insurance industry.

Apartment
Assn.

Paid Brown $5,000 for a speech
in November. Uh... was
reported it paid money for
the Apartment Assn.

Reported lobbying legislation on
rent control and other
landlord-tenant issues.

Pfizer
Pharmaceuticals

Paid $2,435.23 for Brown's
limousine service, dinner and
lodging for a trip to New York.

Reported lobbying state officials
on drug regulation and budget
issues.

Tri-County
Development

Hired Brown as attorney.
Paid him at least $10,000 in
fees.

Reported the possibility of
introducing legislation to locate
a landfill in Solano County.

California Trial
Lawyers Assn.

Paid Brown $3,762 for three
speeches.

Reported lobbying legislation on
more than 240 bills affecting the
legal profession.

California
Applicant
Attorneys Assn.

Paid Brown $5,000 for two
speeches.

Reported lobbying legislation on
two bills to change civil court
contingency limits.

National Medical
Enterprises Inc.

Paid Brown $5,000 for one
speech.

Reported lobbying legislation on
44 bills affecting health care
costs.

California Public
Securities Assn.

Paid Brown $5,000 for one
speech.

Reported lobbying legislation on
seven bills affecting state bonds
and investments.

Chase
Manhattan,
Citicorp and
Chemical banks

Paid $2,058 for dinner for
Brown, on daughter and a
guest in New York.

Reported lobbying legislation on
alternable banking regulation.

Southern
California Edison

Paid Brown $5,100 for one
speech.

Reported lobbying legislation on
more than 200 bills that could
affect the utility company.

California Assn.
of Tobacco &
Candy
Distributors

Paid Brown $3,500 for one
speech.

Reported an interest in 10 bills
affecting tobacco or alcoholic
sales farms.

California Assn.
of Public
Hospitals

Paid Brown $2,750 for one
speech. [illegible] subsequently
[illegible] on the Hospital

Reported lobbying legislation on
29 bills affecting hospitals.

THIS SHOULD
MAKE YOU ANGRY

PHILLIP ABBOTT LUCE

Dear Friend,

I've known Colonel George "Bud" Day since
1973 when we met at Arizona State University
while we were both working for our PHD's.

And I can tell you Colonel Day is an
American you and I can be proud of.

Unlike traitors such as Jane Fonda and Tom
Hayden, Colonel Day served his country with
great honor, bravery and valor.

Colonel Day was awarded this nations
highest tribute, The Congressional Medal of
Honor.

In addition, he has received more military
awards and honors than any American officer
since General Douglas MacArthur, including
50 for combat alone. In fact, Colonel Day is the
most decorated American officer alive today.

And his bravery is unquestioned. Colonel
Day was a prisoner in Viet Nam for 67 months.
He was the only person to ever successfully
escape to the South during the Viet Nam War.

I sincerely hope you will join Colonel Day
and ASFP in our fight to stop Jane Fonda and
Tom Hayden.

I think it's time those anti-American
radicals paid for their treason to our nation.

Sincerely,

Phillip Abbott Luce
President, ASFP

4118-C STREET N.E
WASHINGTON, D.C
20070-0287

Petition
to the
Legislature, State of California

Whereas Assemblyman Tom Hayden did travel to North Viet Nam
as a guest of that nation during a time of open conflict with the United
States and

Whereas Tom Hayden did appear on Hanoi Radio with anti-war
comments aimed directly to American soldiers and

Whereas Tom Hayden did cause American Prisoners of War to meet with
him aiding to the mental anguish and contributing to devastating the
morale of these honorable men and

Whereas an article of the California Constitution specifically states
"No person who advocates the support of a foreign government
against the United States in the event of hostilities shall hold an office
or employment under the State" and

Whereas Tom Hayden is holding the position of Assemblyman in
violation of that article.

Therefore I, the undersigned, do hereby petition the Legislature of
the State of California to remove Tom Hayden from the office of
Assemblyman as a traitor to the United States in accordance with the
State Constitution.

Respectfully Submitted,

A MESSAGE FROM THE COMMANDER

GOVERNOR
PLEASE SIGN THIS
PETITION.

[cartoon image signed by JIM DAVIS]

ADDRESS
PHILLIP ABBOTT LUCE, PRESIDENT
418 A STREET N.E., WASHINGTON, D.C.
20010-0287

You have expressed your concern over the level of political action committee (PAC) involvement in financing election campaigns. You should be aware that in the June primary election there are two measures which would impose some restrictions on campaign contributions. To assist the public in understanding the provisions of these two measures, the Fair Political Practices Commission has just released a comparative analysis. Leidigh Counsel, Legal Division Enclosure cc: Governor's Office Mr James Thompson 10014 S Grevillea Ave Inglewood CA 90304 Governor Deukmejian Your questionnaire on national and state issues - it is our reasoning that you have your hands full to overflowing with state issues I am sending four issues that you should be involved in. These four issues when talked about in public the people get very angry they want something done about it. Thank you James Thompson The handwritten text reads: People Against PACs It's a disgrace that our California State Senate and Assembly is on the auction block, up for grabs to the highest PAC bidders! I want my State Senate and Assembly to support campaign finance reforms that will stop the undue influence PACs have on our representatives. By the way, it is time to show down the dictator Willie Brown. American values, pride not what they used to be Editor: I am not alone when I say the values our country once had are now a dim memory. In my war, we had the policy of "unconditional surrender." We Americans could say in 1946, "We won the war." My father was wounded and gassed on the Western Front in 1918. He earned the Silver Star, the Purple Heart and the Victory Medal. He could say in 1919, "We won the war." No Americans can say that about any war, or anything else, since 1946. What can we say today? We can say our government starts its immigration amnesty program May 5. Know what May 5 is? It's Cinco de Mayo. Welcome illegal aliens. You can have our country. We forgot what it feels like to be real American men. --BILL DREWS San Pedro The article titled "Special Interests a Large Source of Brown's Income" discusses Assembly Speaker Willie Brown's income and the sources of it, particularly from special interests. Here are the main points: **ITEM:** SACRAMENTO — Assembly Speaker
